result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: No, it cannot. The gap is 0.155 vs 0.24. JUSTIFICATION: The Riemann zeta zeros exhibit a higher degree of super-rigidity (lower Delta_3 statistic) than the standard Gaussian Unitary Ensemble (GUE) which describes typical Hermitian matrices. The Delta_3 statistic for zeta zeros is approximately 0.155, whereas for GUE it is approximately 0.24, representing a quantitative gap that prevents a perfect match with standard Hermitian matrix spectra.
